g.text: OPTION A u2014 POLLING PUBLISHER (simple, application-level)
OPTION B u2014 CDC (Change Data Capture, log-based)

text.tspan: WHEN TO CHOOSE WHICH:
u2022 SMALL TEAM, simple needs, modest scale u2192 POLLING PUBLISHER
u2022 LARGE SCALE, low-latency needs, polyglot stack u2192 CDC
u2022 OPERATIONALLY MATURE org running Kafka Connect anyway u2192 CDC
u2022 STARTING OUT u2192 begin with POLLING; migrate to CDC if needed
Either way, the OUTBOX TABLE is the contract:
producer writes there; one of these two mechanisms drains it.
A separate worker process (cron, scheduled job, sidecar)
periodically SELECTs unsent rows and publishes them.
PROS:
u2022 No new infrastructure u2014 just SQL + Kafka client
u2022 Works with any database, any messaging system
u2022 Easy to reason about and operate
u2022 Easy to backfill or replay (just unset sent_at)
CONS:
u2022 Latency = polling interval (typically 100msu20131s)
u2022 Polling load on DB (mitigate: index, LIMIT, partial index on sent_at IS NULL)
u2022 Multiple publishers need leader election or row-locking to avoid duplicates
u2022 Outbox table grows; needs periodic cleanup
A tool (Debezium, AWS DMS, Maxwell, Striim) tails the database's
replication log (Postgres WAL, MySQL binlog, MongoDB oplog) and
pushes inserts into the outbox table directly to Kafka.
PROS:
u2022 Very LOW LATENCY (~milliseconds)
u2022 No DB polling load u2014 reads from replication log
u2022 Naturally ORDERED (replication-log order = commit order)
u2022 Already monitored and operated for DB replication
CONS:
u2022 Extra infrastructure to operate (Debezium, Kafka Connect)
u2022 Tied to DB-specific replication mechanism
u2022 Initial setup complexity (slot management, snapshotting)
u2022 Schema changes can break CDC pipelines
